assertions.description: Every quantitative claim in the input has a corresponding numbered footnote in order of appearance
Footnotes distinguish between direct citations and derived/calculated figures with appropriate language
Source information includes institution name, publication name, date, and URL/file reference

assertion_results.note: Three numbered footnotes matching three claims in order
Footnote 1 says 'as published'; Footnote 2 says 'Calculated by [publication] using regression analysis'
All sources include institution, publication name, date, availability
City Budget Office, FY2025, page 34 cited
Footnotes 1 and 3 marked as derived/calculated; Footnote 2 as direct
European Journalism Centre with edition years and URL cited

methodology: Binary assertion testing: 3 inputs (standard, minimal, European context) x 3 assertions per skill. Each assertion graded pass/fail based on whether generated output satisfies the requirement.
